title: SSA Planning Graph (Full)
description: Pre-merged Planning Graph (nodes + edges) for fast loading and querying inside Suka Smart Assistant.

$schema.description: Optional local schema pointer for editor tooling.

id.description: Identifier for this full Planning Graph definition.

graphId.description: Logical graph id that ties nodes, edges, and full views together.

version.description: Semantic version of the Planning Graph definition.

sourceFiles.description: Optional pointers back to the source node/edge JSON files.

nodes.description: All Planning Graph nodes (calculators, planners, engines, shims, infrastructure, etc.).

edges.description: All Planning Graph edges describing data & flow relationships between nodes.

node.description: A single Planning Graph node representing a calculator, planner, engine, module, or infrastructure helper.

id.description: Stable key used in edges.from / edges.to.

kind.description: Node kind (more permissive in full graph; node-only schema may be stricter).

domain.description: Primary SSA domain this node belongs to.

category.description: Sub-category within the domain (e.g., nutrition, yield, storage).

module.description: Import path for the module implementing this node.

export.description: Named export to call when this node runs.

inputs.description: Named input contracts. Values are type/shape hints (not enforced here).

outputs.description: Named outputs this node produces. Values are type/shape hints (not enforced here).

feedsInto.description: List of node ids this node commonly feeds into (for quick traversal).

tags.description: Free-form tags for grouping, filtering, and UI labeling.

edge.description: A directional relationship between two nodes in the Planning Graph.

weight.description: Relative importance/strength of this relationship. Used for ranking and flow decisions.

meta.description: Additional metadata describing how the edge is used (e.g., via/into mappings).

via.description: Name of the output field / bundle on the source node.

into.description: Name of the input field / bundle on the target node.
